strings.app.name: Palworld Server Manager Custom Edition
strings.settings.languageHelp: Changes every translated label in the app. You can switch anytime.
strings.settings.languagePartial: Some text may still appear in English until this language pack is fully translated.
strings.language.needsReview: This language pack covers every interface label, but still needs review by native speakers.
strings.language.addPackDesc: Only a few languages ship built in. Import a community translation pack (.json) from your computer, or download one by its link.
strings.language.confirmRemove: Remove the "{{name}}" language pack? Its file will be deleted.
strings.language.browseDesc: Ready-made translations hosted on the project's GitHub. Install one with a click — it's checked and validated exactly like an imported file.
strings.language.catalogOffline: Couldn't reach the pack catalog — check your connection, then hit Refresh.
strings.language.noPacks: No community packs are available yet.
strings.language.packLibrary: Browse the pack library on GitHub →
strings.packGuide.title: Language packs
strings.packGuide.subtitle: Create or add your own translation of the app.
strings.packGuide.whatBody: A language pack is a single JSON file that maps every text label in the app to your language. English is always the fallback — any label you leave untranslated simply stays in English, so even a partial pack works.
strings.packGuide.getBody: The Settings → Language screen lists community packs hosted on GitHub — install those with one click. You can also browse the full library on GitHub:
strings.packGuide.step1Body: Download en.json — it lists every key the app uses, each paired with its English text.
strings.packGuide.step2Title: 2. Translate the values, keep the keys
strings.packGuide.step2Body: Change only the text after each colon — never the "key" before it. Keep {{placeholders}} and any HTML tags (like bold) exactly as they are; the app fills and renders them.
strings.packGuide.step3Body: In the meta block set code (e.g. fr or pt-BR), name (English name), nativeName (name in that language) and dir (ltr or rtl).
strings.packGuide.format1: Must be valid JSON — a flat map of text keys to string values.
strings.packGuide.format2: Values must be plain strings; keep any {{placeholders}} and tags intact.
strings.packGuide.format3: Up to 512 KB and 3000 keys. The English (en) base can't be replaced.
strings.packGuide.addBody: Import the .json file from your computer, or paste a direct https link to it. It's validated the same way as a catalog install before it's saved.
strings.packGuide.addedHint: Added {{name}} — pick it in Settings → Language to apply it.
strings.wizard.title: Welcome to Palworld Server Manager
strings.wizard.subtitle: Choose your language to get started.
strings.wizard.help: The manager starts in English. You can change the interface language anytime under Settings → Language.
strings.worlds.title: Your worlds
strings.worlds.communityTip: Listed in the public server browser
strings.worlds.emptyBody: Create your first Palworld world. The manager installs the dedicated server with SteamCMD into a folder you choose, sets up its ports and admin password, and gets it ready to launch.
strings.usage.title: Usage
strings.usage.subtitle: Live CPU & memory for running worlds · {{cores}} cores · sampled every {{sec}}s
strings.usage.emptyBody: Start a world to see live CPU and memory usage graphed here. Metrics are sampled across each server's full process tree.
strings.downloads.title: Downloads & updates
strings.downloads.historyEmpty: Completed installs and updates will appear here.
strings.world.updateStarted: Update started — track progress in the downloads tray
strings.world.noLan: No LAN address detected — the server still listens on all network adapters; use this PC's IP from your OS network settings.
strings.world.connectHelp: In Palworld: Join Multiplayer → Connect via IP and paste an address above. Players on your network use the LAN address; from this same PC use 127.0.0.1. The server isn't bound to any one address — it listens on every adapter on port {{port}}. For friends over the internet, forward/tunnel UDP port {{port}} and share your public IP.
strings.world.playitLink: → Want friends to join over the internet? See the free playit.gg guide
strings.world.deleteIntro: Choose what to remove. This cannot be undone.
strings.world.deleteProfileOnlyDesc: Removes this world from the manager. Server files, saves, and mods on disk are kept — you can re-add the folder later.
strings.world.deleteWithFiles: Delete profile + server files on disk
strings.world.deleteWithFilesDesc: Permanently deletes the entire install folder ({{dir}}) including all saves and backups. There is no recovery.
strings.create.addIntro: Install a fresh Palworld dedicated server, or register a server you already have on disk.
strings.create.installNewDesc: Download the dedicated server with SteamCMD into a folder you choose.
strings.create.useExistingDesc: Point to a PalServer folder you already downloaded. No re-download.
strings.create.installFolderHint: SteamCMD installs the dedicated server (app 2394010) here. Each world needs its own folder.
strings.create.browsePlaceholder: Click Browse to choose a folder
strings.create.typePathToast: Type the full server folder path (native picker is in the desktop app).
strings.create.installStarted: Install started — track progress in the downloads tray
strings.create.adminPasswordHint: Leave blank to auto-generate. Used for REST API + RCON auth.
strings.create.serverFolderHint: Point to the folder containing PalServer.exe (Windows) or PalServer.sh (Linux).
strings.create.browseFolderPlaceholder: Click Browse to choose the folder
strings.create.osWarning: ⚠ This build targets a different OS than this machine.
strings.create.keepPassword: Keep the server's existing admin password (from its settings file)
strings.admin.serverPassword: Server password (players must enter to join)
strings.admin.serverPasswordPlaceholder: Leave blank for an open server (anyone can join)
strings.admin.serverPasswordHint: This is the in-game join password (Palworld's ServerPassword), separate from the admin password. Blank = open server. Restart the world to apply.
strings.admin.communityDesc: Lists this world in Palworld's in-game public server browser so anyone can find and join it. Off means private — friends join by IP only. Adds the -publiclobby launch flag.
strings.admin.communityNote: Note: to actually appear in the list, the server must be reachable from the internet (port-forwarded or tunneled). Restart the world after changing this.
strings.admin.crashRestarted_one: Crash guardian has restarted this world {{count}} time.
strings.admin.crashRestarted_other: Crash guardian has restarted this world {{count}} times.
strings.admin.installFolderDesc: Where this world's server files live. The Mods, saves, and settings are all read from here, so point this at the correct PalServer folder (on any drive). The world must be stopped to change it.
strings.admin.stopToChangeFolder: Stop the world to change its folder.
strings.admin.stopBeforeFolder: Stop the world before changing its install folder.
strings.admin.portsHint: Only the game UDP port needs to be reachable by players (port-forward this one on your router). Keep the REST API port LAN-only. RCON is deprecated by Pocketpair and disabled by default — this manager uses the REST API for all administration.
strings.admin.portsHintStop: Stop the world to change ports.
strings.admin.portsHintNext: Changes take effect the next time this world starts.
strings.admin.stopBeforePorts: Stop the world before changing its ports.
strings.admin.portsUpdated: Ports updated — start the world to apply
strings.logs.waiting: Waiting for output. Logs appear here when the world is running.
strings.backups.empty: No backups yet. Backups zip this world's Saved folder and rotate automatically.
strings.backups.confirmRestore: Restore this backup? A safety backup of the current save is taken first.
strings.backups.importPickerDesktop: Save import via file picker is available in the desktop app.
strings.broadcast.stopBeforeMod: Stop the world before changing the broadcast mod.
strings.broadcast.modInstalledRestart: On-screen broadcast mod installed. Restart the world to load it.
strings.broadcast.modCopiedNoUe4ss: Broadcast mod copied, but UE4SS was not detected — install UE4SS first.
strings.broadcast.confirmRemoveMod: Remove the on-screen broadcast mod? Broadcasts fall back to the chat-feed announce until you reinstall it. Restart the world to fully unload the mod.
strings.broadcast.modRemoved: Broadcast mod removed. Restart the world to unload it.
strings.broadcast.pickFuture: Pick a time in the future
strings.broadcast.wantOnScreenDesc: Right now broadcasts go out through Palworld's REST announce, which shows in the chat feed. For an on-screen system message, add the bundled broadcast mod — it needs UE4SS (the Lua mod loader), which isn't installed on this world yet.
strings.broadcast.stopToChangeMod: Stop the world to change the broadcast mod.
strings.broadcast.enableDesc: UE4SS is installed. Add the bundled broadcast mod to show messages on every player's screen, then restart the world to load it. Until then, broadcasts appear in the chat feed.
strings.broadcast.stopToInstall: Stop the world to install the broadcast mod.
strings.broadcast.modOnRest: — messages show as the on-screen server notice while the world runs.
strings.broadcast.stopToRemoveMod: Stop the world to remove the broadcast mod
strings.broadcast.noneScheduled: No scheduled broadcasts. They fire once at the set time, then disappear. If the app is closed past the time, the broadcast is kept and flagged as missed.
strings.broadcast.missedLine: Missed — was due {{time}}. Reschedule it or send it now.
strings.broadcast.footerModOn: Broadcasts show on-screen via the installed mod (the server's on-screen notice). The red pre-shutdown countdown look is exclusive to actual shutdowns and can't be triggered for a normal message.
strings.broadcast.footerModOff: Broadcasts currently go out as Palworld's REST announce (chat feed). Install the on-screen broadcast mod above to show them on players' screens instead.
strings.info.title: Info & Guides
strings.info.subtitle: Optional help for getting the most out of your server.
strings.info.guideInternetTitle: How to host your server on the internet
strings.info.guideInternetSubtitle: Make your world joinable by friends anywhere — free, no port forwarding
strings.info.whatIsPlayitDesc: A free tunneling service that gives your local server a public address, so friends can join over the internet without port forwarding or router changes. This is a recommendation, not a requirement — if you already have port forwarding set up, you don't need it.
strings.info.step1: Go to playit.gg, create a free account, and download the playit agent for Windows.
strings.info.step2: Run the downloaded agent and sign in / link it to your account when the browser opens. Leave it running.
strings.info.step3: In the playit.gg dashboard, add a new tunnel.
strings.info.step4: Palworld uses UDP. Choose the UDP tunnel type on the free tier.
strings.info.step5: You only need a single port for one world, so set the port allocation to 1.
strings.info.step6Intro: Point the tunnel at your machine:
strings.info.step6LocalPort: Local port: your world's game port (e.g. 8211 — shown on the world page)
strings.info.step7: playit.gg gives you a public IP and port. Share that with friends — in Palworld they use Join Multiplayer → Connect via IP and paste it. Done!
strings.settings.title: Settings
strings.settings.discordMovedDesc: Discord webhooks are now set per world, so each server can post to its own channel. Open a world → Discord tab to add its webhook, choose which events to announce, and toggle chat relay. Any webhook you had here before has been cleared.
strings.settings.chatCaptureDesc: Capture in-game chat via the UE4SS relay mod. Turn this off to stop the app from reading chat entirely. If a Palworld update ever makes the mod misbehave, also use Remove chat mod on each world's Chat tab to take the mod off the server.
strings.settings.keepLastN: Keep last N backups per world
strings.settings.backupLocationDesc: Backups are ZIP snapshots of each world's Saved folder, stored outside the server so a game update can't touch them. Currently saving to {{where}}:
strings.settings.backupPathPlaceholder: Leave blank for the default location
strings.settings.existingBackupsNote: Existing backups stay where they are — only new backups go to the new folder.
strings.settings.steamcmdNote: — installed automatically on first world provisioning.
strings.common.pickerDesktop: File picker is available in the desktop app.
strings.common.folderPickerDesktop: Folder picker is available in the desktop app.
strings.players.name: Name
strings.chat.stopBeforeChange: Stop the world before changing the chat mod.
strings.chat.installedRestart: Chat mod installed. Restart the world to start capturing chat.
strings.chat.copiedNoUe4ss: Chat mod copied, but UE4SS was not detected — install UE4SS first.
strings.chat.confirmRemove: Remove the chat relay mod from this server? Chat capture stops until you reinstall it. Restart the world to fully unload the mod.
strings.chat.removedRestart: Chat relay mod removed. Restart the world to unload it.
strings.chat.captureOffDesc: The in-game chat capture feature is disabled globally. Turn it back on in <s>Settings</s> to read chat again.
strings.chat.needsUe4ssDesc: In-game chat is captured by a small UE4SS Lua mod. UE4SS (the Lua mod loader) isn’t installed on this world yet — install it first, then come back and add the chat relay mod.
strings.chat.stopToChange: Stop the world to change the chat mod.
strings.chat.installRelayDesc: UE4SS is installed. Add the bundled chat relay mod, then restart the world to start capturing chat.
strings.chat.stopToInstall: Stop the world to install the chat mod.
strings.chat.modInstalledRest: — player messages appear here live while the world runs.
strings.chat.stopToRemove: Stop the world to remove the chat mod
strings.chat.empty: No chat yet. In-game player messages appear here live while the world is running.
strings.chat.startToCapture: Start the world to capture chat.
strings.chat.announcePlaceholder: Broadcast an announcement to all players…
strings.chat.footer: Palworld has no REST endpoint to send as a player, so admin messages go out as a server announcement (broadcast to everyone).
strings.schedule.playerFilterHint: The name box picks whose join fires this — leave it blank to fire for anyone.
strings.schedule.systemHint: Sent as a System announcement in the in-game chat feed.
strings.schedule.onscreenHint: Shown on players' screens (needs the on-screen notice mod; falls back to chat).
strings.schedule.playerTokenHint: Use {player} to insert the player's name.
strings.schedule.onscreenNeedsModTitle: On-screen notices need the broadcast mod
strings.schedule.onscreenNeedsModDesc: This world doesn't have the on-screen broadcast mod set up yet, so scheduled notices will fall back to the chat feed. Set it up on the Broadcast tab to show them on players' screens.
strings.schedule.empty: No scheduled jobs. Add automatic restarts, backups, updates, or timed messages on a maintenance window.
strings.warn.title: Warn players before restart / update
strings.warn.desc: Broadcasts a countdown in-game before a scheduled or manual restart/update, then hands off to Palworld's native red shutdown countdown for the final minute. Requires the REST API (on by default).
strings.warn.hint: Set Repeat every to 0 (or ≥ the start time) to warn just once. Example: start 10, repeat 2 → notices at 10, 8, 6, 4, 2 minutes, then the red countdown.
strings.warn.defaultMessage: The server will restart in {minutes} minute(s). Please get to a safe place.
strings.mods.confirmRemove: Remove mod "{{pkg}}"? Its files will be deleted.
strings.mods.windowsOnly: Palworld server-side mods run on the Windows dedicated server only. This host isn't Windows, so mods may not load even if configured here.
strings.mods.runningNotice: The world is running. Stop it to add, enable/disable, or remove mods — these changes only take effect at boot.
strings.mods.bootNotice: Mods load only at server boot — restart the world after any change. On restart, Palworld deploys each active mod per its Info.json rules.
strings.mods.globalEnabled: Enabled — active mods will load on next start.
strings.mods.workshopPlaceholder: Steam Workshop ID (already downloaded in Steam)
strings.mods.workshopHelpAria: How to find a Workshop ID
strings.mods.autoDetected_one: Auto-detected {{count}} Steam library. Set a folder only if your mods aren't found.
strings.mods.autoDetected_other: Auto-detected {{count}} Steam libraries. Set a folder only if your mods aren't found.
strings.mods.noSteamDetected: No Steam install auto-detected. If Steam isn't on C:, set your Steam (or SteamLibrary) folder here.
strings.mods.empty: No mods installed. Import a Workshop-style mod (.zip containing Info.json), or add one you've subscribed to in Steam by its Workshop ID.
strings.mods.danglingNotice: These mods are listed as active but their files aren't on disk: {{list}}. Re-import them or disable them.
strings.mods.wsModalIntro: PSM adds a Workshop mod from the copy Steam has already downloaded on this PC — it doesn't download it for you. So the order is: subscribe in Steam first, then add it here by its ID.
strings.mods.wsStep1: Subscribe in Steam. Open the mod on the Palworld Steam Workshop and click Subscribe. Steam downloads it into your Steam library's workshop folder.
strings.mods.wsStep2: Copy its Workshop ID. It's the number at the end of the mod's URL — steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1234567890. The number is the ID.
strings.mods.wsStep3: Paste the ID into the field and click Add. PSM finds it across your Steam libraries (any drive) and installs it into this world.
strings.mods.wsModalNote: No Steam copy? Use Import mod (.zip) instead — pick a mod archive that contains an Info.json and PSM will install it directly, no subscription needed.
strings.discord.testSent: Test message sent — check your Discord channel
strings.discord.notificationsDesc: Add one or more Discord webhook channels for this world, then choose which channel each event posts to below — e.g. a Status channel for start/stop/crash, a separate Backup channel, and a Chat channel for the in-game relay.
strings.discord.noChannels: No channels yet. Add one to start posting notifications.
strings.discord.routeDesc: Pick the channel each event posts to, or Don't send to mute it.
strings.discord.chatNeedsMod: Needs the chat relay mod installed on the Chat tab.
strings.discord.channelNoUrl: This channel has no webhook URL — nothing will be sent.
strings.discord.relayDesc: Post this world's captured in-game chat to the webhook above for a Palworld→Discord feed. Needs the chat relay mod installed on the Chat tab.
strings.ue4ss.stopBeforeInstall: Stop the world before installing UE4SS.
strings.ue4ss.installedRestart: UE4SS installed — restart the world to load it.
strings.ue4ss.installedNotDetected: Install ran but UE4SS was not detected.
strings.ue4ss.confirmRemove: Remove UE4SS mod "{{name}}"? Its files will be deleted.
strings.ue4ss.title: UE4SS / Lua mods
strings.ue4ss.desc: UE4SS runs Lua script mods (most Palworld mods on Nexus). This is separate from the Steam Workshop system above. Mods install under your server's UE4SS mods folder (Pal\Binaries\Win64\ue4ss\Mods on UE4SS 3.0).
strings.ue4ss.notInstalledTitle: UE4SS isn’t installed for this world
strings.ue4ss.notInstalledDesc: Download the UE4SS release zip (the dedicated-server build) from its official page, then install it here. The app extracts it into Win64 and sets the dedicated-server-safe options for you.
strings.ue4ss.consoleEnabledDesc: GuiConsoleVisible is on, which crashes a dedicated server on launch. Turn it off.
strings.ue4ss.stopToManage: Stop the world to add, enable/disable, or remove Lua mods.
strings.ue4ss.loadsAtBoot: UE4SS only loads mods at boot.
strings.ue4ss.emptyMods: No UE4SS mods yet. Import a Lua mod (.zip containing Scripts/main.lua).
strings.ue4ss.restartNote: Mods load at server boot — restart the world after any change.
strings.editor.presetApplied: Applied "{{name}}" preset — review and Save changes.
strings.editor.noFileNotice: No settings file yet — showing defaults. Saving will create PalWorldSettings.ini.
strings.editor.onlyChangedNotice: Only the settings you change are written — everything else keeps Palworld's own value. Changes apply after a restart.
strings.editor.defaultTitle: Not written in your ini — using Palworld default
strings.editor.communityNotice: Public IP/port only work once this server is listed. Turn on Community server in Admin.
strings.editor.iniEditorTitle: Edit the raw PalWorldSettings.ini with version history
strings.ini.discardConfirm: You have unsaved changes to the ini. Discard them?
strings.ini.savedRestart: Saved — restart the world to apply
strings.ini.restoredRestart: Restored — restart the world to apply
strings.ini.runningNotice: The world is running. Palworld rewrites this file on shutdown — use the app's Restart to apply edits safely.
strings.ini.noFileNotice: No saved ini yet — showing the shipped defaults. Saving will create the file.
strings.ini.noVersions: No versions yet. Saving creates restore points.
strings.ini.loadedIntoEditor: Loaded into editor — Save to keep
strings.customize.accentColorHint: — colors the card edge, page top bar & default avatar
strings.customize.footer: Images are resized automatically to keep things light. Banner shows on the world card (fading in from the right) and at the top of this world's page.
strings.reserved.title: Reserved slots
strings.reserved.managerModeHelp: Keeps public occupancy below the configured limit. Enforcement will only be activated after the preview has been verified.
strings.reserved.name: Name
strings.playit.title: Playit.gg Center
strings.playit.help: Configure the public endpoint advertised to Palworld's community browser. The server continues to listen on its local game port.
strings.playit.stopBeforeSave: Stop the world before changing Playit settings.
